reasoning: Virgin Galactic (SPCE) has sufficient cash runway to easily make it to 2026 without filing for bankruptcy. As of the end of Q2 2025, the company had $508 million in cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities. Their quarterly free cash flow burn is currently guided at around $100M to $115M, meaning they should end 2025 with $300M+ in cash. Their major debt maturity ($425 million in convertible senior notes) is not due until February 2027, alleviating near-term restructuring pressure. Furthermore, a 1-for-20 reverse split successfully kept the company listed on the NYSE, and their stock remains actively traded with a market cap around $300 million as of late 2025. Therefore, a sudden, unexpected bankruptcy filing within the next 53 days (before January 1, 2026) is extremely unlikely.
